CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-12-2016 Continued To Mar-23-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE
Off Calendar Jan-07-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS GOLDEN PACIFIC BANK, N.A., VIRGINIA VARELA, RICK FOWLER, ANTHONY RUSSO, DAVID ROCHE AND KELLY WONG'S MOTION TO CHANGE VENUE is granted. The amended complaint filed by Plaintiff alleges causes of action against a corporation and individual defendants. An individual defendant has a right upon a proper showing to a change of venue to the county of his residence even though venue as initially laid may otherwise be justifiable upon one of the four alternative grounds provided by section 395.5 in actions against corporations. (Carruth v. Superior Court (1978) 80 Cal.App.3d 215, 221.) In cases with mixed causes of action, a motion for change of venue must be granted on the entire complaint if the defendant is entitled to a change on any one cause of action. (Brown v. Superior Court (1984) 37 Cal.3d 477, 488.) Defendants have presented evidence which establishes that at the commencement of the lawsuit, San Francisco was not the principal place of business of Golden Pacific Bank, N.A. and that none of the defendants resided in San Francisco. (See Declarations of Virginia Varela, Rick Fowler, Anthony Russo, David Roche, and Kelly Wong in Support of Defendants' Motion to Change Venue.) Furthermore, Defendants have submitted sufficient evidence to show that Defendant Virginia Varela resided in Sacramento, California at the commencement of the lawsuit and that she continues to reside in Sacramento, California. (Declaration of Virginia Varela in Support of Defendants' Motion to Change Venue; Supplemental Declaration of Virginia Varela in Support of Reply to Plaintiff's Opposition to Defendants' Motion to Change Venue.) Because Defendant Virginia Varela resided in Sacramento, California at the commencement of the lawsuit, the court grants Defendants' motion to change venue. (CCP section 395; See Carruth v. Superior Court (1978) 80 Cal.App.3d 215.) Order signed.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489. (302/EG)

Plaintiff?S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer And Consolidate Cases
Off Calendar Nov-10-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. WILLIAM WEBB, ESQ. of Webb Legal, Tel No. 415-277-7200 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. BRUCE A. SCHEIDT, ESQ. of Kronick Moskovitz Tiedmann & Girard, Tel No. 916-321-4500 appearing for Defendant Golden Pacific Bank, N.A.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopts its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF BILLFLOAT, INC.'S Motion To Transfer And Consolidate Cases is denied. Plaintiff's motion to transfer and consolidate cases is denied because Plaintiff has not established that common questions of law and fact predominate in both of the actions as required by California Civil Procedure sections 403 and 404.1. Although the action filed in the Sacramento Superior Court includes a breach of contract claim involving the same contract that is at issue in the instant action, that case raises separate issues and claims which are distinct from the issues raised in the instant case. For example, as part of its breach of contract cause of action, Golden Pacific Bank alleges that BillFloat, Inc. breached the Software License Agreement by failing to comply with Regulation B of the Equal Credit Opportunity Act and the Fair Credit Reporting Act. In the separate action, Golden Pacific Bank also alleges that BillFloat, Inc. breached the contract by sharing intellectual property without its consent and by refusing to provide Golden Pacific Bank with the opportunity to agree on a delivery and payment schedule for the SmartBiz Brand SOW. Additionally, the action names two defendants who are not parties in the instant action and raises causes of action alleging fraud, misappropriation of trade secrets, and a breach of a separate non-disclosure agreement. The presence of the additional claims and issues in the Sacramento Superior Court filed action indicates that common questions of law and fact do not predominate in both cases. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

Defendant Notice Of Motion And Motion To Change Venue
Off Calendar Nov-10-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. WILLIAM WEBB, ESQ. of Webb Legal, Tel No. 415-277-7200 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. BRUCE A. SCHEIDT, ESQ. of Kronick Moskovitz Tiedmann & Girard, Tel No. 916-321-4500 appearing for Defendant Golden Pacific Bank, N.A.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT GOLDEN PACIFIC BANK, N.A.'S Motion To Change Venue is denied. Under California Civil Procedure section 395.5, in a case which raises a contract claim against a corporation defendant, the corporation may be sued in the county where the contract was made or was to be performed, or where the obligation or liability arose, or where the breach occurred, or in the county where the corporation maintains its principal place of business. (California Civil Procedure section 395.5) The "place of making of a contract is where the last act necessary to the validity and binding effect thereof is performed. The 'last act' is usually the acceptance of the offer." (Mitchell v. Superior Court (1986) 186 Cal.App.3d 1040, 1045-1046.) Based on the evidence submitted, the relevant offer in this case was made on July 18, 2014, when Defendant's CEO, Virginia Varela e-mailed a copy of the contract that she had signed to Plaintiff and on July 22, 2014, when Defendant made payment to Plaintiff of the $320,749 Invoice Amount. Acceptance of the offer occurred on July 24, 2014, when Plaintiff signed the contract and sent it to Ms. Varela. (O'Malley Decl., paragraphs 12-15.) Plaintiff signed and emailed the contract to Ms. Valera from his office in San Francisco. Therefore, because acceptance occurred in San Francisco, the contract was executed in San Francisco, and San Francisco is a proper venue for the case. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
